articles.title: Security Council LIVE: Mounting attacks and Russian threats prompt emergency Ukraine meeting
articles.summary: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session on Ukraine, with Secretary-General António Guterres telling ambassadors following what Kyiv describes as the most devastating attack on its capital to date, that “the time for peace is now.” In the early hours of 23-24 May, Russia launched a massive barrage of missiles and drones against multiple Ukrainian cities. Moscow has since threatened further sustained strikes. Sharp divisions among Council members are expected, with European nations

articles.title: Malicious Sicoob NuGet Steals Banking Credentials as npm Packages Target Cloud Secrets
articles.summary: Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a malicious NuGet package that masquerades as a C# software development kit for Sicoob, one of Brazil's largest cooperative financial systems, to siphon client IDs and PFX certificates. articles.summary: The North Korean state-sponsored threat actor known as Kimsuky (aka Velvet Chollima) has been attributed to a fresh set of cyber attacks targeting South Korean military and corporate entities through March and April 2026. articles.title: UN warns Ukraine war risks spiralling ‘out of control’
articles.summary: The United Nations on Thursday warned of a dangerous escalation in the war in Ukraine after a wave of large-scale Russian strikes and threats of further attacks, with Secretary-General António Guterres saying “the death spiral must stop.”

articles.title: New Russian-Linked GREYVIBE Targets Ukraine with AI-Powered Cyberattacks
articles.summary: A previously undocumented threat actor dubbed GREYVIBE has been attributed to ongoing and persistent attacks targeting Ukraine and Ukraine-related entities since at least August 2025. articles.title: Critical Gogs RCE Vulnerability Lets Any Authenticated User Execute Arbitrary Code
articles.summary: A critical security vulnerability has been disclosed in Gogs, a popular open-source self-hosted Git service, that allows an authenticated user to execute arbitrary code under certain conditions. articles.summary: Romania will hold an emergency meeting following the incident, in which two people were injured.

articles.summary: A 21-year-old Austrian man is found guilty of planning the attack and other terrorism-related offences.

articles.title: Global temperatures set to stay near record levels: UN weather agency
articles.summary: The world is heading into another period of dangerous heat, a new UN report warns on Thursday, and it’s nearly certain global temperatures over the next five years will stay “at or near record levels” as climate change accelerates across land and sea.

articles.title: Ukraine: Russian attack destroys humanitarian food aid in Dnipro
articles.summary: The World Food Programme (WFP) has deplored a Russian attack on a warehouse in Dnipro, Ukraine that destroyed “a significant quantity” of food aid destined for thousands in frontline areas.

articles.title: NASA Uses Mineralogical Marker to Understand Ancient Martian Climate
articles.summary: Scientists analyzed 20 Martian samples collected by NASA’s Curiosity Rover and found that differences in hematite crystallite size at varying elevations could serve as a new mineralogical marker for understanding Mars’ ancient climate.

articles.title: Eight students arrested in Kenya after suspected deadly school arson attack

articles.title: Ebola outbreak in DR Congo collides with conflict and hunger, WHO warns
articles.summary: The UN World Health Organization (WHO) on Wednesday warned that eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo faces a “catastrophic collision of disease and conflict” as a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak outpaces containment efforts in a region already battered by armed violence, mass displacement and acute hunger.

articles.title: Hubble Captures M88 on Journey to Center of Virgo Cluster
articles.summary: This NASA Hubble Space Telescope image features the active spiral galaxy Messier 88 (M88), located about 63 million light-years away.

articles.title: Grandoreiro Malware and BTMOB RAT Campaigns Target Windows and Android Users
articles.summary: Latin America and Europe become the target of two banking trojan campaigns that are designed to infect Windows and Android devices with Grandoreiro and BTMOB malware, respectively. That's according to new findings from WatchGuard and ESET, which have observed the two malware families being used to single out companies in Spain, Portugal, and Mexico, as well as mobile users in Brazil. articles.title: Exploding rocket casts doubts over Nasa's Moon plans
articles.summary: Explosion of Blue Origin rocket is a setback for the company and for Nasa's Moon plans.

articles.title: Survival before safety for Delhi's poor as temperatures hit 45C
articles.summary: As temperatures peak, Delhi’s informal workers continue working despite growing health risks.

articles.title: Italy restores lucky testicles on bull mosaic worn down by tourists
articles.summary: The artwork is the centre of a tradition that involves visitors performing three spins on it for good fortune.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Lebanon violence continues, Israel ‘severs ties’ with UN chief, Internal displacement in Haiti, warning to France over prison overcrowding
articles.summary: The UN has expressed deep concern over escalating hostilities in Lebanon following intensified Israeli airstrikes in southern Beirut and across southern parts of the country.

articles.title: World News in Brief: Sudan and Haiti updates, Afghan women’s rights
articles.summary: The United Nations is raising alarm over continued drone attacks in Sudan’s Darfur region after multiple strikes reportedly killed civilians and intensified fears for communities already trapped by the brutal conflict between rival militaries.

articles.title: Wars and geopolitical divisions constitute ‘dangerous erosion’ of world order, warns UN chief
articles.summary: The UN Charter is facing one of its gravest tests in decades, Secretary-General António Guterres told the Security Council on Tuesday, warning that wars, arms races, climate shocks and the erosion of international law are placing intense pressure on the multilateral system established to prevent a third world war.

articles.title: Painting the Growing Season in the Maize Triangle
articles.summary: Radar data from an agricultural area in South Africa, shown in a vivid color palette, reveal crop types and how they changed during the Southern Hemisphere’s growing season.
